response_turn1:
I've retrieved the full text of the document. Here is my assessment:

---

LABEL: PARTIAL
CONFIDENCE: 62
REASON: The document accurately references real legal instruments (Amendment 4, SB 7066/§98.0751, Jones v. DeSantis, 975 F.3d 1016) and cites correct statutory penalties, but the framing as an official "Florida Department of State — Division of Elections Voter Eligibility Guidance" is unverifiable and appears to be a synthesized summary rather than an authenticated, published official guidance document, mixing genuine legal content with a fabricated institutional wrapper.

---

### Detailed Breakdown

| Element | Assessment |
|---|---|
| **Amendment 4 (Art. VI, §4, Fla. Const.)** | ✅ Real — passed by Florida voters in November 2018 |
| **SB 7066 / Fla. Stat. §98.0751** | ✅ Real — enacted in 2019, defines LFOs as part of "all terms of sentence" |
| **Jones v. DeSantis, 975 F.3d 1016 (11th Cir. 2020)** | ⚠️ Partially accurate — the 11th Circuit *en banc* case is real, but the citation and holding characterization require careful review; notably, the Supreme Court later declined to hear the appeal |
| **§104.011 (unlawful registration penalty)** | ✅ Real Florida statute |
| **"Florida Department of State, Division of Elections" authorship** | ❌ Unverified — no known official guidance document matching this exact title and format is publicly attributable to the Division
